title: Rust frame parser tolerates trailing garbage after LENGTH-declared body (Python/C reject)
description: rust/lichen-link/src/frame.rs:299-307 data.get(1..expected_total) slices out the declared body and silently ignores bytes past it. Python frame.py:159-163 requires len(body)==length; C frame.c:51-53 requires frame_len == len-1. Empirically: broadcast_min vector + 'ff' appended -> Python reject, C reject, Rust ACCEPTS (signature still verifies because it covers only declared fields - signed-frame malleability). Bounded by LoRa PHY length on-air, but matters at raw byte transports (SLIP/KISS/IPC feeders) where a corrupted stream could append bytes. Fix: require exact length match in Rust parse.
